mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in fewer than 50% of rate-decline months.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 6.0 percent, labor market slack signals weakening consumer demand and corporate earnings deterioration, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 65% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 6.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital expenditure growth, generating positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter as earnings expectations rise.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase reflects capacity utilization improvements and margin expansion, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over twenty trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 20-day returns during recovery phases, with out-of-sample accuracy below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 255.0, accelerating inflation triggers expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns within five trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL readings above 255.0 are followed by positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total nonfarm payrolls and falling unemployment rates confirms robust job creation absorbing labor slack, supporting corporate revenue growth and positive S&P 500 returns over sixty trading days.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence signal yields negative 60-day returns in more than 58% of triggered months.

mechanism_narrative: Consecutive quarterly declines in real GDP signal a contractionary demand shock that forces earnings downgrades, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon during recessionary transitions.
failure_condition: GDP contractions fail to predict negative 20-day returns, with signal accuracy dropping below 50% in recessionary quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in the index of industrial production reflect immediate supply-side momentum and inventory restocking cycles, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO changes are followed by negative 5-day returns in more than 55% of cases across all market conditions.
